patterns.name: Source → Extract → Normalize → Score → Store pipeline
patterns.description: A linear data pipeline where records are pulled from an external source, parsed into a structured format, cleaned, ranked by configurable criteria, and persisted. This pattern recurs across any workflow that replaces manual data research or enrichment.

patterns.name: Configurable Logic Layer
patterns.description: A rules engine or criteria layer that sits between raw data and output, allowing operators to adjust what gets included, excluded, or ranked without modifying the pipeline infrastructure.

patterns.name: Scheduled Repeatable Workflow
patterns.description: A workflow designed to run on a recurring cadence without human intervention, replacing a process that was previously executed manually or delegated to an external party.

patterns.name: Human-Workflow Replacement
patterns.description: Automating a workflow that was previously performed by a human or outsourced service, shifting ownership and execution speed to the operator while eliminating recurring service costs.

patterns.name: Downstream Delivery Adapter
patterns.description: A final step in the pipeline that formats and routes output to any consuming system — CRM, spreadsheet, webhook, or dashboard — without changing upstream pipeline logic.
